name: anubis (by osiris
shortDescription: Anubis is the ancient Egyptian god of embalming and the dead, guiding souls into the afterlife. He is often depicted as a jackal-headed figure or as a wild dog.
description: Anubis, or Inpu in ancient Egyptian, held a vital role in the funerary rites and the journey of souls into the afterlife. He was primarily responsible for embalming the dead, ensuring their bodies were preserved for the afterlife. His jackal head is believed to be associated with the wild dogs that frequented cemeteries, scavenging for remains, though it also may symbolize protection. According to some myths, Anubis was the illegitimate son of Nephthys and Osiris, conceived when Nephthys tricked Osiris into sleeping with her by disguising herself as his wife, Isis. Fearing Set's wrath, Nephthys abandoned Anubis, who was then found and raised by Isis. Anubis is credited with inventing the process of mummification to preserve Osiris' body after Set murdered him. Anubis played a crucial role in the Weighing of the Heart ceremony, where the deceased's heart was weighed against the feather of Ma'at (truth and justice). If the heart was lighter than the feather, the soul could proceed to the afterlife; if heavier, it would be devoured by the demon Ammit. He guided souls through the underworld, protecting them from dangers and ensuring their safe passage. Anubis's significance lessened somewhat with the rise of Osiris as the primary god of the underworld, but he remained an important figure in funerary rituals and beliefs.
epithets: He Who is Upon His Mountain

sources.title: The Egyptian Book of the Dead
sources.author: Various Scribes
